description:
You are an edge-computing engineer who has just received an automatically-generated inventory file that lists the current connectivity status of several IoT devices in the field.  
    Your goal is to create a concise, machine-readable connection report so that the deployment pipeline can automatically halt if too many devices are offline.

    Directory /home/user/edge_devices already exists and contains two sub-items:  
      • /home/user/edge_devices/devices.json     (the inventory you must read)  
      • /home/user/edge_devices/reports/         (an empty directory you will place the report in)

    1. Read /home/user/edge_devices/devices.json.  
       The file is a JSON array in which every object has at least the keys “id”, “ip”, and “status”.  
       The “status” field can be either "online" or "offline".

    2. From that JSON you must compute:  
       • TOTAL – total number of devices present.  
       • ON   – count of devices whose status is "online".  
       • OFF  – count of devices whose status is "offline".  
       • LIST – a single comma-separated string containing the ids of all offline devices, sorted lexicographically (ASCII sort), with no embedded spaces.

    3. Create the report file /home/user/edge_devices/reports/connection_summary.log with **exactly five non-empty lines** and **no trailing blank lines** in the following strict format (each bullet corresponds to an entire line in the file):

       1) an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp to second precision in the form YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  
       2) Total devices: <TOTAL>  
       3) Online devices: <ON>  
       4) Offline devices: <OFF>  
       5) Offline list: <LIST>

       Example of correct formatting (values will differ in your run):

           2025-04-29T16:30:25Z
           Total devices: 17
           Online devices: 15
           Offline devices: 2
           Offline list: cam-03,sensor-18

       Notes on formatting that the automated grader will enforce:  
       • The timestamp must end with “Z”.  
       • There must be exactly one space after each colon in lines 2-4.  
       • “Offline list:” is followed by one space, then the comma-separated list, with no spaces inside the list itself.  
       • Do not append any color codes or extra commentary to the file.

    4. Display the full contents of /home/user/edge_devices/reports/connection_summary.log to stdout so that a human reviewer (and the test harness) can quickly inspect it.

    Hints (do NOT echo the commands themselves in the report):  
      • Using `jq` or another JSON-processing tool will make extracting fields straightforward.  
      • GNU `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` prints the required UTC timestamp format.

    The automated test will confirm that:
      • /home/user/edge_devices/reports/connection_summary.log exists.  
      • The file contains exactly five lines in the prescribed order and format.  
      • The numeric values and offline list are correct with respect to devices.json.  
      • The timestamp line matches the ISO-8601 UTC pattern.

    Produce your solution inside a single shell session. You should not need more than three or four shell commands to finish the task.
truth:
Initial state supplied to the container before the agent starts:

    1. File /home/user/edge_devices/devices.json  
       Contents (5 lines, compact array, no final newline):  
       [
         {"id":"sensor-1","ip":"10.0.0.11","status":"online"},
         {"id":"sensor-2","ip":"10.0.0.12","status":"offline"},
         {"id":"gateway-1","ip":"10.0.0.1","status":"online"},
         {"id":"camera-1","ip":"10.0.0.20","status":"offline"},
         {"id":"sensor-3","ip":"10.0.0.13","status":"online"}
       ]

       Parsed counts:
         TOTAL = 5
         ON    = 3
         OFF   = 2
         LIST  = camera-1,sensor-2   (lexicographically sorted)

    2. Directory /home/user/edge_devices/reports/ exists and is empty.  
       It is writable by the unprivileged user running the agent.

    Expected artefact after the agent finishes:

    • /home/user/edge_devices/reports/connection_summary.log  
      Five lines; the second through fifth lines must read exactly:

          Total devices: 5
          Online devices: 3
          Offline devices: 2
          Offline list: camera-1,sensor-2

      Line 1 is a timestamp that matches regex  
          ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$  

    The test harness:
      • Verifies existence and readability of the report file.  
      • Splits into lines, checks there are exactly 5.  
      • Validates line 1 against the timestamp regex above.  
      • Confirms lines 2-5 match the exact strings listed.  
      • Ensures no extra lines or trailing whitespace exist.
